_comment: Cross-impl byte-parity fixture for the age-claim canonical signing form v1 (#1623, child-B contract). 'canonical_utf8' is the exact byte string both the Go server (age.Claim.CanonicalBytes) and the child-B WebCrypto signer MUST produce from 'claim'. 'signature_b64' + 'public_key_spki_b64' are backfilled in Task 4 with a committed test keypair so child B can verify byte+signature parity. The nonce here is a low-entropy placeholder; the Task-4 backfill uses a real high-entropy nonce (allowlisted in .secrets.baseline).
canonical_utf8:
age-claim/v1
canonical_version=1
user_id=11111111-1111-4111-8111-111111111111
valid_age=true
nsfw_auth=false
jurisdiction_obligation=1
nonce=deadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeefdeadbeef
timestamp=1750000000
key_version=1
client_version=0.2.0
salt_length: PSSSaltLengthEqualsHash (= 32, SHA-256 length) — child B MUST use salt length 32; PSSSaltLengthAuto fails verify
